topic: Geographic Perspectives: Global Change (Population Distribution, Global Climate, Global Resource Consumption)
subject: geography

items.q: Why has the Richter scale been largely replaced by the moment magnitude scale?
items.correct: Richter saturates above magnitude ~7 and was calibrated for local Californian quakes; moment magnitude (Mw) measures the total energy released and works for all sizes globally.
items.misconception: The Richter scale is still the standard scale used by all seismologists.
items.random_wrong: Moment magnitude measures damage while Richter measures shaking.
items.why: Media still says 'Richter' but seismologists now report Mw. Both are logarithmic — a magnitude 7 releases ~32x more energy than a 6 — but Mw is the modern standard for large quakes.

items.correct: The combined loss of water from a surface through evaporation (from soil and water bodies) and transpiration (from plant leaves).
items.misconception: It is only water evaporating from puddles and lakes.
items.random_wrong: It is the process of rain falling from clouds.
items.why: Students focus only on evaporation. Transpiration from forests can move enormous quantities of water — a key concept for understanding deforestation's effect on the water cycle.

items.q: Why do meanders migrate across a floodplain?
items.correct: Faster water on the outside of the bend causes erosion (river cliff); slower water on the inside causes deposition (slip-off slope) — over time the channel shifts laterally.
items.misconception: Meanders form because rivers flow around obstacles like rocks.
items.random_wrong: Meanders form because the river is going downhill very steeply.
items.why: Meanders form on gentle gradients, not steep ones, and self-organise rather than dodging obstacles. Students project an intuitive 'water goes around things' model onto a fluid dynamic process.
It's Crutzen and Stoermer. “Lovelock and Margulis” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many planetary boundaries did Rockstrom et al identify in 2009?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 9. “12” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Roughly what percentage of global internet traffic uses submarine cables?
items.why: Exact answer: 99 percent. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
